tags.description: Japanese Navy seaplane tender sunk 24 September 1944. Wreck lies on port side in 36m, with upper surfaces at 22m. Advanced wreck diving required.
tags.name: Akitsushima

tags.description: Also known as South Miniloc, this signature El Nido site features vibrant coral gardens with large schools of yellow snappers, tunas, jacks, and barracudas. Shaped like a triangle at 12m depth. Best conditions April-October with mild currents.
tags.name: Biet Point

tags.description: El Nido cave dive with natural passages and chambers. Advanced qualification required for cave diving.
tags.name: Cave

tags.description: Southern tip of Matinloc Island in El Nido. Excellent site for pelagic fishes with strong currents attracting tuna, jacks, and mackerels. Coral-encrusted rocks provide shelter and color.
tags.name: Banayan Point

tags.description: Matinloc Island site (Picanayas) with large boulders attracting whitetip sharks. Pelagics commonly observed. Strong currents bring robust marine life.
tags.name: Bikanayos Rock

tags.description: Port Barton area. Shallow coral reef ideal for snorkeling and beginners. Excellent coral formations with butterflyfish, clownfish, and colorful reef fish.
tags.name: Twin Reef

tags.description: Port Barton. Dramatic coral formations with massive brain corals. Schools of fish cruise the site. Drop-offs visible in calm seas. Good intermediate skill level site.
tags.name: Fantastic Reef

tags.description: Part of Tubbataha Reef Marine Park, south Palawan. Mushroom-shaped reef structure with sloping reefs and dramatic undercut drop-offs. Strong shark activity including grey reef sharks, whitetips, and hammerheads. Barracuda, Spanish mackerel, and Napoleon wrasse. Accessible March-June only.
tags.name: Jesse Beazley Reef
